1	`timescale 1ns / 1ps
2	
3	module nco_400m_enhanced (
4	    input wire clk_400m,
5	    input wire reset_n,
6	    input wire [31:0] frequency_tuning_word,
7	    input wire phase_valid,
8	    input wire [15:0] phase_offset,
9	    output reg signed [15:0] sin_out,
10	    output reg signed [15:0] cos_out,
11	    output reg dds_ready
12	);
13	
14	// ============================================================================
15	// 6-stage pipelined NCO for 400 MHz timing closure
16	//
17	// Stage 1: Phase accumulator update (DSP48E1 in P=P+C mode)
18	//          DSP48E1 does: P_reg <= P_reg + C_port (frequency_tuning_word)
19	//          The P register output IS the phase accumulator — no CARRY4 chain.
20	//          phase_accum_reg <= P_output[31:0] (fabric register captures DSP output)
21	// Stage 2: Offset addition in fabric (registered)
22	//          phase_with_offset <= phase_accum_reg + {phase_offset, 16'b0}
23	//          Breaking DSP→CARRY4 into two registered stages eliminates the
24	//          critical path (was -0.594ns WNS in Build 6)
25	// Stage 3a: Register LUT address (lut_index) and quadrant from phase_with_offset
26	//           Only 2 registers driven (minimal fanout, short routes)
27	// Stage 3b: LUT read using registered lut_index → register abs values + quadrant
28	//           Registered LUT address → combinational LUT6 read → register
29	//           Eliminates the routing-dominant critical path (-0.100ns in Build 8)
30	// Stage 4: Compute negations from registered abs values → register neg values
31	//          (CARRY4 x4 chain has registered inputs, fits in 2.5ns easily)
32	// Stage 5: Quadrant sign application → sin_out, cos_out (pure MUX, no arith)
33	//
34	// Total latency: 6 cycles from phase_valid to sin/cos output
35	// Max logic levels per stage: Stage 1=DSP48E1(internal), Stage 2=4(CARRY4x5),
36	//   Stage 3a=1(LUT3 quadrant+index decode), Stage 3b=1(LUT6 ROM read),
37	//   Stage 4=4(CARRY4 chain), Stage 5=1(MUX)
38	// ============================================================================
39	
40	// Phase accumulator — DSP48E1 P output provides the accumulated phase
41	// In simulation: behavioral reg. In synthesis: DSP48E1 P[31:0].
42	reg [31:0] phase_accum_reg;     // Stage 1 output: registered DSP48E1 P[31:0]
43	reg [31:0] phase_with_offset;   // Stage 2 output: phase_accum_reg + offset
44	
45	// Stage 3a pipeline registers: registered LUT address + quadrant
46	reg [5:0] lut_index_pipe_sin;
47	reg [5:0] lut_index_pipe_cos;
48	reg [1:0] quadrant_pipe;
49	
50	// Stage 3b pipeline registers: LUT output + quadrant
51	reg [15:0] sin_abs_reg, cos_abs_reg;
52	reg [1:0] quadrant_reg;
53	
54	// Stage 4 pipeline registers: pre-computed negations + abs copies + quadrant
55	reg signed [15:0] sin_neg_reg, cos_neg_reg;
56	reg [15:0] sin_abs_reg2, cos_abs_reg2;  // Pass-through for Stage 5 MUX
57	reg [1:0] quadrant_reg2;                 // Pass-through for Stage 5 MUX
58	
59	// Valid pipeline: tracks 6-stage latency
60	reg [5:0] valid_pipe;
61	
62	// Use only the top 8 bits for LUT addressing (256-entry LUT equivalent)
63	wire [7:0] lut_address = phase_with_offset[31:24];
64	
65	// Quarter-wave sine LUT (0-90 degrees only)
66	// Force distributed RAM (LUTRAM) — the 64x16 LUT is only 1024 bits, far too
67	// small for BRAM. BRAM CLK→DOADO delay (2.454ns) + downstream negation logic
68	// (1.236ns) exceeded the 2.5ns period at 400 MHz (WNS = -2.238ns). LUTRAM
69	// read is combinatorial (~0.5ns through LUTs), giving the Stage 2→3 negation
70	// path ~2.1ns of budget which fits comfortably.
71	(* ram_style = "distributed" *) reg [15:0] sin_lut [0:63]; // 64 entries for 0-90 degrees
72	
73	// Initialize sine LUT
74	integer lut_init_i;
75	initial begin
76	    for (lut_init_i = 0; lut_init_i < 64; lut_init_i = lut_init_i + 1) begin
77	        sin_lut[lut_init_i] = 16'h0000;
78	    end
79	    
80	    // Initialize quarter-wave sine LUT (0-90 degrees)
81	    // LUT[k] = round(32767 * sin(pi/2 * k / 64)), monotonically increasing
82	    // FIX: Original LUT peaked at index 42 then decreased — broke cos=sin[63-k] quadrature
83	    sin_lut[0] = 16'h0000; sin_lut[1] = 16'h0324; sin_lut[2] = 16'h0648; sin_lut[3] = 16'h096A;
84	    sin_lut[4] = 16'h0C8C; sin_lut[5] = 16'h0FAB; sin_lut[6] = 16'h12C8; sin_lut[7] = 16'h15E2;
85	    sin_lut[8] = 16'h18F9; sin_lut[9] = 16'h1C0B; sin_lut[10] = 16'h1F1A; sin_lut[11] = 16'h2223;
86	    sin_lut[12] = 16'h2528; sin_lut[13] = 16'h2826; sin_lut[14] = 16'h2B1F; sin_lut[15] = 16'h2E11;
87	    sin_lut[16] = 16'h30FB; sin_lut[17] = 16'h33DF; sin_lut[18] = 16'h36BA; sin_lut[19] = 16'h398C;
88	    sin_lut[20] = 16'h3C56; sin_lut[21] = 16'h3F17; sin_lut[22] = 16'h41CE; sin_lut[23] = 16'h447A;
89	    sin_lut[24] = 16'h471C; sin_lut[25] = 16'h49B4; sin_lut[26] = 16'h4C3F; sin_lut[27] = 16'h4EBF;
90	    sin_lut[28] = 16'h5133; sin_lut[29] = 16'h539B; sin_lut[30] = 16'h55F5; sin_lut[31] = 16'h5842;
91	    sin_lut[32] = 16'h5A82; sin_lut[33] = 16'h5CB3; sin_lut[34] = 16'h5ED7; sin_lut[35] = 16'h60EB;
92	    sin_lut[36] = 16'h62F1; sin_lut[37] = 16'h64E8; sin_lut[38] = 16'h66CF; sin_lut[39] = 16'h68A6;
93	    sin_lut[40] = 16'h6A6D; sin_lut[41] = 16'h6C23; sin_lut[42] = 16'h6DC9; sin_lut[43] = 16'h6F5E;
94	    sin_lut[44] = 16'h70E2; sin_lut[45] = 16'h7254; sin_lut[46] = 16'h73B5; sin_lut[47] = 16'h7504;
95	    sin_lut[48] = 16'h7641; sin_lut[49] = 16'h776B; sin_lut[50] = 16'h7884; sin_lut[51] = 16'h7989;
96	    sin_lut[52] = 16'h7A7C; sin_lut[53] = 16'h7B5C; sin_lut[54] = 16'h7C29; sin_lut[55] = 16'h7CE3;
97	    sin_lut[56] = 16'h7D89; sin_lut[57] = 16'h7E1D; sin_lut[58] = 16'h7E9C; sin_lut[59] = 16'h7F09;
98	    sin_lut[60] = 16'h7F61; sin_lut[61] = 16'h7FA6; sin_lut[62] = 16'h7FD8; sin_lut[63] = 16'h7FF5;
99	end
100	
101	// Combinational: quadrant determination and LUT index (feeds Stage 3a registers)
102	wire [1:0] quadrant_w = lut_address[7:6];
103	wire [5:0] lut_index = (quadrant_w[0] ^ quadrant_w[1]) ? ~lut_address[5:0] : lut_address[5:0];
104	
105	// Combinational LUT read using REGISTERED lut_index_pipe (feeds Stage 3b registers)
106	// These wires are driven by lut_index_pipe (registered in Stage 3a), so the
107	// combinational path is just: lut_index_pipe_reg → LUT6 (distributed RAM read)
108	// This eliminates the LUT3→LUT6 two-level critical path from Build 8.
109	wire [15:0] sin_abs_w = sin_lut[lut_index_pipe_sin];
110	wire [15:0] cos_abs_w = sin_lut[63 - lut_index_pipe_cos];
111	
112	// ============================================================================
113	// Stage 1: Phase accumulator (DSP48E1) — accumulates FTW each cycle
114	// Stage 2: Offset addition in fabric — breaks DSP→CARRY4 critical path
115	//
116	// The phase accumulator is the critical path bottleneck: a 32-bit addition
117	// requires 8 CARRY4 stages in fabric (2.826 ns > 2.5 ns budget at 400 MHz).
118	// Solution: Use DSP48E1 in P = P + C accumulate mode.
119	//   - C-port carries frequency_tuning_word (zero-extended to 48 bits)
120	//   - CREG=1 registers the tuning word inside the DSP
121	//   - PREG=1 registers the accumulator output (P = P + C each cycle)
122	//   - The DSP48E1 48-bit ALU performs the add internally at full speed
123	//   - Only P[31:0] is used (32-bit phase accumulator)
124	//
125	// Phase offset addition is split into a separate pipeline stage:
126	//   Stage 1: phase_accum_reg <= P[31:0]  (just capture the DSP output)
127	//   Stage 2: phase_with_offset <= phase_accum_reg + {phase_offset, 16'b0}
128	// This eliminates the DSP48E1.P→CARRY4 chain critical path (-0.594ns in Build 6).
129	// ============================================================================
130	
131	`ifdef SIMULATION
132	// ---- Behavioral model for Icarus Verilog simulation ----
133	// Mimics DSP48E1 accumulator: P <= P + C, with CREG=1, PREG=1
134	// Stage 1: phase_accum_reg captures accumulator output
135	// Stage 2: phase_with_offset adds phase offset
136	reg [31:0] phase_accumulator;
137	
138	always @(posedge clk_400m or negedge reset_n) begin
139	    if (!reset_n) begin
140	        phase_accumulator <= 32'h00000000;
141	        phase_accum_reg   <= 32'h00000000;
142	        phase_with_offset <= 32'h00000000;
143	    end else if (phase_valid) begin
144	        // Stage 1: accumulate + capture
145	        phase_accumulator <= phase_accumulator + frequency_tuning_word;
146	        phase_accum_reg   <= phase_accumulator;
147	        // Stage 2: offset addition (uses previous cycle's phase_accum_reg)
148	        phase_with_offset <= phase_accum_reg + {phase_offset, 16'b0};
149	    end
150	end
151	
152	`else
153	// ---- DSP48E1 phase accumulator for Vivado synthesis ----
154	// P = P + C mode: accumulates frequency_tuning_word each clock cycle
155	// Uses 1 DSP48E1 (total design: 5 of 240 available = 2.08%)
156	wire [47:0] phase_accum_p;          // DSP48E1 P output (48 bits, use [31:0])
157	
158	DSP48E1 #(
159	    // Feature control
160	    .A_INPUT("DIRECT"),
161	    .B_INPUT("DIRECT"),
162	    .USE_DPORT("FALSE"),
163	    .USE_MULT("NONE"),           // No multiplier — pure ALU accumulate
164	    .USE_SIMD("ONE48"),
165	    // Pipeline registers
166	    .AREG(0),                    // A-port unused for accumulate
167	    .BREG(0),                    // B-port unused for accumulate
168	    .CREG(1),                    // Register frequency_tuning_word on C-port
169	    .MREG(0),                    // No multiplier
170	    .PREG(1),                    // P register IS the phase accumulator
171	    .ADREG(0),
172	    .ACASCREG(0),
173	    .BCASCREG(0),
174	    .ALUMODEREG(0),
175	    .CARRYINREG(0),
176	    .CARRYINSELREG(0),
177	    .DREG(0),
178	    .INMODEREG(0),
179	    .OPMODEREG(0),
180	    // Pattern detector (unused)
181	    .AUTORESET_PATDET("NO_RESET"),
182	    .MASK(48'h3fffffffffff),
183	    .PATTERN(48'h000000000000),
184	    .SEL_MASK("MASK"),
185	    .SEL_PATTERN("PATTERN"),
186	    .USE_PATTERN_DETECT("NO_PATDET")
187	) dsp_phase_accum (
188	    // Clock and reset
189	    .CLK(clk_400m),
190	    .RSTA(1'b0),
191	    .RSTB(1'b0),
192	    .RSTM(1'b0),
193	    .RSTP(!reset_n),             // Reset P register (phase accumulator) on !reset_n
194	    .RSTC(!reset_n),             // Reset C register (tuning word) on !reset_n
195	    .RSTALLCARRYIN(1'b0),
196	    .RSTALUMODE(1'b0),
197	    .RSTCTRL(1'b0),
198	    .RSTD(1'b0),
199	    .RSTINMODE(1'b0),
200	    // Clock enables
201	    .CEA1(1'b0),
202	    .CEA2(1'b0),
203	    .CEB1(1'b0),
204	    .CEB2(1'b0),
205	    .CEC(1'b1),                  // Always register C (tuning word updates)
206	    .CEM(1'b0),
207	    .CEP(phase_valid),           // Only accumulate when phase_valid is asserted
208	    .CEAD(1'b0),
209	    .CEALUMODE(1'b0),
210	    .CECARRYIN(1'b0),
211	    .CECTRL(1'b0),
212	    .CED(1'b0),
213	    .CEINMODE(1'b0),
214	    // Data ports
215	    .A(30'b0),                   // Unused for P = P + C
216	    .B(18'b0),                   // Unused for P = P + C
217	    .C({16'b0, frequency_tuning_word}),  // Zero-extend 32-bit FTW to 48 bits
218	    .D(25'b0),
219	    .CARRYIN(1'b0),
220	    // Control ports
221	    .OPMODE(7'b0101100),         // Z=P (010), Y=C (11), X=0 (00) → P = P + C
222	    .ALUMODE(4'b0000),           // Z + X + Y + CIN (standard add)
223	    .INMODE(5'b00000),
224	    .CARRYINSEL(3'b000),
225	    // Output ports
226	    .P(phase_accum_p),
227	    .PATTERNDETECT(),
228	    .PATTERNBDETECT(),
229	    .OVERFLOW(),
230	    .UNDERFLOW(),
231	    .CARRYOUT(),
232	    // Cascade ports (unused)
233	    .ACIN(30'b0),
234	    .BCIN(18'b0),
235	    .CARRYCASCIN(1'b0),
236	    .MULTSIGNIN(1'b0),
237	    .PCIN(48'b0),
238	    .ACOUT(),
239	    .BCOUT(),
240	    .CARRYCASCOUT(),
241	    .MULTSIGNOUT(),
242	    .PCOUT()
243	);
244	
245	// Stage 1: Capture DSP48E1 P output into fabric register
246	// Stage 2: Add phase offset to captured value
247	// Split into two registered stages to break DSP48E1.P→CARRY4 critical path
248	always @(posedge clk_400m or negedge reset_n) begin
249	    if (!reset_n) begin
250	        phase_accum_reg   <= 32'h00000000;
251	        phase_with_offset <= 32'h00000000;
252	    end else if (phase_valid) begin
253	        // Stage 1: just capture DSP output (no CARRY4 chain)
254	        phase_accum_reg   <= phase_accum_p[31:0];
255	        // Stage 2: offset add (CARRY4 chain from registered fabric→fabric, easy timing)
256	        phase_with_offset <= phase_accum_reg + {phase_offset, 16'b0};
257	    end
258	end
259	
260	`endif
261	
262	// ============================================================================
263	// Stage 3a: Register LUT address and quadrant from phase_with_offset
264	//           Only 2 registers driven (lut_index_pipe + quadrant_pipe)
265	//           Minimal fanout → short routes → easy timing
266	// ============================================================================
267	always @(posedge clk_400m or negedge reset_n) begin
268	    if (!reset_n) begin
269	        lut_index_pipe_sin <= 6'b000000;
270	        lut_index_pipe_cos <= 6'b000000;
271	        quadrant_pipe      <= 2'b00;
272	    end else if (valid_pipe[1]) begin
273	        lut_index_pipe_sin <= lut_index;
274	        lut_index_pipe_cos <= lut_index;
275	        quadrant_pipe      <= quadrant_w;
276	    end
277	end
278	
279	// ============================================================================
280	// Stage 3b: LUT read using registered lut_index_pipe + register abs values
281	//           Registered address → combinational LUT6 read → register
282	//           Only 1 logic level (LUT6), trivial timing
283	// ============================================================================
284	always @(posedge clk_400m or negedge reset_n) begin
285	    if (!reset_n) begin
286	        sin_abs_reg <= 16'h0000;
287	        cos_abs_reg <= 16'h7FFF;
288	        quadrant_reg <= 2'b00;
289	    end else if (valid_pipe[2]) begin
290	        sin_abs_reg <= sin_abs_w;
291	        cos_abs_reg <= cos_abs_w;
292	        quadrant_reg <= quadrant_pipe;
293	    end
294	end
295	
296	// ============================================================================
297	// Stage 4: Compute negations from registered abs values
298	//          CARRY4 x4 chain has registered inputs — easily fits in 2.5ns
299	//          Also pass through abs values and quadrant for Stage 5
300	// ============================================================================
301	always @(posedge clk_400m or negedge reset_n) begin
302	    if (!reset_n) begin
303	        sin_neg_reg <= 16'h0000;
304	        cos_neg_reg <= -16'h7FFF;
305	        sin_abs_reg2 <= 16'h0000;
306	        cos_abs_reg2 <= 16'h7FFF;
307	        quadrant_reg2 <= 2'b00;
308	    end else if (valid_pipe[3]) begin
309	        sin_neg_reg <= -sin_abs_reg;
310	        cos_neg_reg <= -cos_abs_reg;
311	        sin_abs_reg2 <= sin_abs_reg;
312	        cos_abs_reg2 <= cos_abs_reg;
313	        quadrant_reg2 <= quadrant_reg;
314	    end
315	end
316	
317	// ============================================================================
318	// Stage 5: Quadrant sign application → final sin/cos output
319	//          Uses pre-computed negated values from Stage 4 — pure MUX, no arithmetic
320	// ============================================================================
321	always @(posedge clk_400m or negedge reset_n) begin
322	    if (!reset_n) begin
323	        sin_out <= 16'h0000;
324	        cos_out <= 16'h7FFF;
325	    end else if (valid_pipe[4]) begin
326	        case (quadrant_reg2)
327	            2'b00: begin // Quadrant I: sin+, cos+
328	                sin_out <= sin_abs_reg2;
329	                cos_out <= cos_abs_reg2;
330	            end
331	            2'b01: begin // Quadrant II: sin+, cos-
332	                sin_out <= sin_abs_reg2;
333	                cos_out <= cos_neg_reg;
334	            end
335	            2'b10: begin // Quadrant III: sin-, cos-
336	                sin_out <= sin_neg_reg;
337	                cos_out <= cos_neg_reg;
338	            end
339	            2'b11: begin // Quadrant IV: sin-, cos+
340	                sin_out <= sin_neg_reg;
341	                cos_out <= cos_abs_reg2;
342	            end
343	        endcase
344	    end
345	end
346	
347	// ============================================================================
348	// Valid pipeline and dds_ready (6-stage latency)
349	// ============================================================================
350	always @(posedge clk_400m or negedge reset_n) begin
351	    if (!reset_n) begin
352	        valid_pipe <= 6'b000000;
353	        dds_ready <= 1'b0;
354	    end else begin
355	        valid_pipe <= {valid_pipe[4:0], phase_valid};
356	        dds_ready <= valid_pipe[5];
357	    end
358	end
359	
360	// Debug verification of LUT initialization (simulation only)
361	`ifdef SIMULATION
362	initial begin
363	    #10;
364	    $display("NCO: Sine LUT initialized with %0d entries", 64);
365	end
366	`endif
367	
368	endmodule
369

content.content:
1	module cic_decimator_4x_enhanced (
2	    input wire clk,                 // 400MHz input clock
3	    input wire reset_n,
4	    input wire signed [17:0] data_in,  // 18-bit input
5	    input wire data_valid,
6	    output reg signed [17:0] data_out, // 18-bit output
7	    output reg data_out_valid,       // Valid at 100MHz
8	    // Enhanced monitoring outputs
9	    output reg saturation_detected,  // Latched saturation indicator
10	    output reg [7:0] max_value_monitor, // For gain control
11	    input wire reset_monitors        // Clear saturation and max value
12	);
13	
14	parameter STAGES = 5;
15	parameter DECIMATION = 4;
16	parameter COMB_DELAY = 1;
17	
18	// Accumulator width: DSP48E1 native 48-bit.
19	// CIC uses modular (wrapping) arithmetic so extra MSBs are harmless.
20	localparam ACC_WIDTH = 48;
21	
22	// Comb section operates on 28-bit (18 + 5*log2(4) = 28, exact for comb range).
23	localparam COMB_WIDTH = 28;
24	
25	// ============================================================================
26	// INTEGRATOR CHAIN — explicit DSP48E1 with PCOUT→PCIN cascade
27	// ============================================================================
28	// Integrator[0]: P = P + C,    C = sign_extend(data_in)  [from fabric]
29	// Integrator[k]: P = P + PCIN, PCIN from integrator[k-1] [dedicated cascade]
30	//
31	// The PCOUT→PCIN cascade uses dedicated silicon routing between vertically
32	// adjacent DSP48E1 tiles — zero fabric delay, guaranteed to meet 400+ MHz
33	// on 7-series regardless of speed grade.
34	//
35	// Active-high reset derived from reset_n (inverted).
36	// CEP (clock enable for P register) gated by data_valid.
37	// ============================================================================
38	
39	wire reset_h = ~reset_n;  // active-high reset for DSP48E1 RSTP
40	
41	// Sign-extended input for integrator_0 C port (48-bit)
42	wire [ACC_WIDTH-1:0] data_in_c = {{(ACC_WIDTH-18){data_in[17]}}, data_in};
43	
44	// DSP48E1 cascade wires
45	wire [47:0] pcout_0, pcout_1, pcout_2, pcout_3;
46	wire [47:0] p_out_0, p_out_1, p_out_2, p_out_3, p_out_4;
47	
48	// Comb stage 0 DSP48E1 output wire (CREG+AREG+BREG pipelined subtract)
49	wire [47:0] comb_0_p_out;
50	
51	// ============================================================================
52	// SHARED REGISTER DECLARATIONS
53	// ============================================================================
54	// These registers are referenced by both the synthesis DSP48E1 instances
55	// (inside `ifndef SIMULATION) and the behavioral simulation model (inside
56	// `else), as well as the shared fabric logic (after `endif).
57	// Icarus Verilog 13.0 requires registers to be declared before their first
58	// use within any `ifdef branch, so we declare them here — before the
59	// `ifndef SIMULATION block — rather than in the post-`endif shared section.
60	// ============================================================================
61	(* keep = "true", dont_touch = "true" *) reg signed [COMB_WIDTH-1:0] integrator_sampled;
62	(* keep = "true", dont_touch = "true", max_fanout = 1 *) reg signed [COMB_WIDTH-1:0] integrator_sampled_comb;
63	(* use_dsp = "yes" *) reg signed [COMB_WIDTH-1:0] comb [0:STAGES-1];
64	reg signed [COMB_WIDTH-1:0] comb_delay [0:STAGES-1][0:COMB_DELAY-1];
65	
66	// Pipeline valid for comb stages 1-4: delayed by 1 cycle vs comb_pipe to
67	// account for CREG+AREG+BREG pipeline inside comb_0_dsp (explicit DSP48E1).
68	// Comb[0] result appears 1 cycle after data_valid_comb_pipe.
69	(* keep = "true", max_fanout = 16 *) reg data_valid_comb_0_out;
70	
71	// Enhanced control and monitoring
72	reg [1:0] decimation_counter;
73	(* keep = "true", max_fanout = 16 *) reg data_valid_delayed;
74	(* keep = "true", max_fanout = 16 *) reg data_valid_comb;
75	(* keep = "true", max_fanout = 16 *) reg data_valid_comb_pipe;
76	reg [7:0] output_counter;
77	reg [ACC_WIDTH-1:0] max_integrator_value;
78	reg overflow_detected;
79	reg overflow_latched;
80	
81	// Diagnostic registers
82	reg [7:0] saturation_event_count;
83	reg [31:0] sample_count;
84	
85	// Comb-stage saturation flags
86	reg comb_overflow_latched;
87	reg comb_saturation_detected;
88	reg [7:0] comb_saturation_event_count;
89	
90	// Temporary signals for calculations
91	reg signed [ACC_WIDTH-1:0] abs_integrator_value;
92	reg signed [COMB_WIDTH-1:0] temp_scaled_output;
93	reg signed [17:0] temp_output;
94	
95	// Pipeline stage for saturation comparison
96	reg sat_pos;
97	reg sat_neg;
98	reg signed [17:0] temp_output_pipe;
99	reg data_out_valid_pipe;
100	
101	integer i, j;
102	
103	`ifndef SIMULATION
104	// ============================================================================
105	// SYNTHESIS: Explicit DSP48E1 instances with PCOUT→PCIN cascade
106	// ============================================================================
107	
108	// --- Integrator 0: P = P + C (accumulate sign-extended input) ---
109	// OPMODE = 7'b0101100: Z=P(010), Y=C(11), X=0(00) → P = P + C
110	// CREG=1: C port is registered inside DSP48E1. This eliminates the
111	// fabric→DSP C-port setup timing violation (-0.415ns in Build 6).
112	// The CREG adds 1 cycle of latency before data reaches the ALU.
113	// CEC=data_valid gates the C register to match CEP behavior.
114	DSP48E1 #(
115	    .A_INPUT            ("DIRECT"),
116	    .B_INPUT            ("DIRECT"),
117	    .USE_DPORT          ("FALSE"),
118	    .USE_MULT           ("NONE"),
119	    .AUTORESET_PATDET   ("NO_RESET"),
120	    .MASK               (48'h3FFFFFFFFFFF),
121	    .PATTERN             (48'h000000000000),
122	    .SEL_MASK           ("MASK"),
123	    .SEL_PATTERN        ("PATTERN"),
124	    .USE_PATTERN_DETECT ("NO_PATDET"),
125	    .ACASCREG           (0),
126	    .ADREG              (0),
127	    .ALUMODEREG         (0),
128	    .AREG               (0),
129	    .BCASCREG           (0),

484	// ============================================================================
485	// COMB STAGE 0 — Explicit DSP48E1 with CREG=1 for Critical Path Fix
486	// ============================================================================
487	// Build 18 critical path: integrator_sampled_comb_reg → comb_reg[0]/C[38]
488	//   WNS = +0.062 ns, data path = 1.022 ns (0.379 logic + 0.643 route)
489	//
490	// By enabling CREG=1 (+ AREG=1, BREG=1), the fabric register
491	// integrator_sampled_comb is absorbed into the DSP48's internal C pipeline
492	// register, eliminating the 0.643 ns fabric→DSP routing delay entirely.
493	// The DSP48 performs: P = C_reg - {A_reg, B_reg}  (i.e., subtract)
494	//
495	// Latency: +1 cycle vs. the old inferred comb[0]. This is accounted for
496	// by the data_valid_comb_0_out signal, which delays the valid for stages 1-4.
497	//
498	// C-port = sign-extended integrator_sampled_comb (28→48 bits)
499	// A:B    = sign-extended comb_delay[0][0] (28→48 bits)
500	// OPMODE = 7'b0110011: Z=C(011), Y=0(00), X=A:B(11)
501	// ALUMODE= 4'b0011:    Z - (X + Y + CIN) = C - A:B
502	//
503	// The comb_delay[0][0] register stays in fabric (captures
504	// integrator_sampled_comb at the same time as the C register, unchanged).
505	// Comb stages 1-4 remain inferred with (* use_dsp = "yes" *).
506	
507	// Sign-extended inputs for comb_0 DSP48E1
508	wire [47:0] comb_0_c_in = {{(48-COMB_WIDTH){integrator_sampled_comb[COMB_WIDTH-1]}},
509	                            integrator_sampled_comb};
510	wire [47:0] comb_0_ab_in = {{(48-COMB_WIDTH){comb_delay[0][COMB_DELAY-1][COMB_WIDTH-1]}},
511	                             comb_delay[0][COMB_DELAY-1]};
512	
513	DSP48E1 #(
514	    .A_INPUT            ("DIRECT"),
515	    .B_INPUT            ("DIRECT"),
516	    .USE_DPORT          ("FALSE"),
517	    .USE_MULT           ("NONE"),
518	    .AUTORESET_PATDET   ("NO_RESET"),
519	    .MASK               (48'h3FFFFFFFFFFF),
520	    .PATTERN             (48'h000000000000),
521	    .SEL_MASK           ("MASK"),
522	    .SEL_PATTERN        ("PATTERN"),
523	    .USE_PATTERN_DETECT ("NO_PATDET"),
524	    .ACASCREG           (1),       // A cascade register matches AREG
525	    .ADREG              (0),
526	    .ALUMODEREG         (0),
527	    .AREG               (1),       // A-port registered — eliminates fabric routing
528	    .BCASCREG           (1),       // B cascade register matches BREG
529	    .BREG               (1),       // B-port registered — eliminates fabric routing
530	    .CARRYINREG         (0),
531	    .CARRYINSELREG      (0),
532	    .CREG               (1),       // *** KEY: C-port registered inside DSP48 ***
533	                                   // Absorbs integrator_sampled_comb FDRE, eliminates
534	                                   // 0.643 ns fabric→DSP C-port routing delay.
535	    .DREG               (0),
536	    .INMODEREG          (0),
537	    .MREG               (0),
538	    .OPMODEREG          (0),
539	    .PREG               (1)        // P register enabled (output pipeline)
540	) comb_0_dsp (
541	    .CLK                (clk),
542	    // A:B = sign-extended comb_delay[0][last] (subtrahend)
543	    .A                  (comb_0_ab_in[47:18]),   // Upper 30 bits
544	    .B                  (comb_0_ab_in[17:0]),    // Lower 18 bits
545	    .C                  (comb_0_c_in),           // integrator_sampled_comb (minuend)
546	    .D                  (25'd0),
547	    .CARRYIN            (1'b0),
548	    .CARRYINSEL         (3'b000),
549	    .OPMODE             (7'b0110011),  // Z=C, Y=0, X=A:B → ALU input = C, A:B
550	    .ALUMODE            (4'b0011),     // Z - (X+Y+CIN) = C - A:B
551	    .INMODE             (5'b00000),
552	    .CEA1               (1'b0),
553	    .CEA2               (data_valid_comb_pipe),  // Load A register when valid
554	    .CEB1               (1'b0),
555	    .CEB2               (data_valid_comb_pipe),  // Load B register when valid
556	    .CEC                (data_valid_comb_pipe),  // Load C register when valid
557	    .CED                (1'b0),
558	    .CEM                (1'b0),
559	    .CEP                (1'b1),        // Always propagate — P updates 1 cycle after
560	                                       // input registers are loaded
561	    .CEAD               (1'b0),
562	    .CEALUMODE          (1'b0),
563	    .CECTRL             (1'b0),
564	    .CECARRYIN          (1'b0),
565	    .CEINMODE           (1'b0),
566	    .RSTP               (reset_h),
567	    .RSTA               (reset_h),
568	    .RSTB               (reset_h),
569	    .RSTC               (reset_h),
570	    .RSTD               (1'b0),
571	    .RSTM               (1'b0),
572	    .RSTALLCARRYIN      (1'b0),
573	    .RSTALUMODE         (1'b0),
574	    .RSTCTRL            (1'b0),
575	    .RSTINMODE          (1'b0),
576	    .P                  (comb_0_p_out),
577	    .PCOUT              (),
578	    .ACOUT              (),
579	    .BCOUT              (),
580	    .CARRYCASCOUT       (),
581	    .CARRYOUT           (),
582	    .MULTSIGNOUT        (),
583	    .OVERFLOW           (),
584	    .PATTERNBDETECT     (),
585	    .PATTERNDETECT      (),
586	    .UNDERFLOW          ()
587	);
588	
589	`else
590	// ============================================================================
591	// SIMULATION: Behavioral model (Icarus Verilog compatible)
592	// ============================================================================
593	// Functionally identical: each integrator is P <= P + input, gated by data_valid.
594	// integrator_0 adds sign-extended data_in; stages 1-4 add previous stage output.
595	//
596	// CREG=1 on integrator_0: The C-port register adds 1 cycle of latency.
597	// data_in_c_delayed models this: on cycle N with data_valid, the DSP's C register
598	// captures data_in_c(N), but the ALU uses the PREVIOUS C register value.
599	// So sim_int_0 accumulates data_in_c_delayed (1 cycle behind data_in_c).
600	// ============================================================================
601	reg signed [ACC_WIDTH-1:0] sim_int_0, sim_int_1, sim_int_2, sim_int_3, sim_int_4;
602	reg signed [ACC_WIDTH-1:0] data_in_c_delayed;  // Models CREG=1 on integrator_0
603	
604	// Comb_0 DSP48E1 behavioral model (models CREG+AREG+BREG+PREG pipeline)
605	// In simulation there is no DSP48E1 primitive, so we model the 4-stage pipe:
606	//   Stage 1 (CREG/AREG/BREG): capture C and A:B inputs (on data_valid_comb_pipe)
607	//   Stage 2 (PREG): P = C_reg - AB_reg (always, like CEP=1 in synthesis)
608	reg signed [COMB_WIDTH-1:0] sim_comb_0_c_reg;   // Models CREG
609	reg signed [COMB_WIDTH-1:0] sim_comb_0_ab_reg;  // Models AREG+BREG (combined)
610	reg signed [47:0] sim_comb_0_p_reg;              // Models PREG
611	
612	always @(posedge clk) begin
613	    if (reset_h) begin
614	        sim_int_0 <= 0;
615	        sim_int_1 <= 0;
616	        sim_int_2 <= 0;
617	        sim_int_3 <= 0;
618	        sim_int_4 <= 0;
619	        data_in_c_delayed <= 0;
620	        sim_comb_0_c_reg <= 0;
621	        sim_comb_0_ab_reg <= 0;
622	        sim_comb_0_p_reg <= 0;
623	    end else begin
624	        if (data_valid) begin
625	            // CREG pipeline: capture current data, use previous
626	            data_in_c_delayed <= $signed(data_in_c);
627	            sim_int_0 <= sim_int_0 + data_in_c_delayed;
628	            sim_int_1 <= sim_int_1 + sim_int_0;
629	            sim_int_2 <= sim_int_2 + sim_int_1;
630	            sim_int_3 <= sim_int_3 + sim_int_2;
631	            sim_int_4 <= sim_int_4 + sim_int_3;
632	        end
633	        // Comb_0 DSP48 behavioral model:
634	        // CREG/AREG/BREG load on data_valid_comb_pipe (like CEC/CEA2/CEB2)
635	        if (data_valid_comb_pipe) begin
636	            sim_comb_0_c_reg <= integrator_sampled_comb;
637	            sim_comb_0_ab_reg <= comb_delay[0][COMB_DELAY-1];
638	        end
639	        // PREG always updates (CEP=1): P = C_reg - AB_reg
640	        sim_comb_0_p_reg <= {{(48-COMB_WIDTH){sim_comb_0_c_reg[COMB_WIDTH-1]}}, sim_comb_0_c_reg}
641	                          - {{(48-COMB_WIDTH){sim_comb_0_ab_reg[COMB_WIDTH-1]}}, sim_comb_0_ab_reg};
642	    end
643	end
644	
645	assign comb_0_p_out = sim_comb_0_p_reg;
646	
647	assign p_out_0 = sim_int_0;
648	assign p_out_1 = sim_int_1;
649	assign p_out_2 = sim_int_2;
650	assign p_out_3 = sim_int_3;
651	assign p_out_4 = sim_int_4;
652	// pcout wires unused in simulation
653	assign pcout_0 = sim_int_0;
654	assign pcout_1 = sim_int_1;
655	assign pcout_2 = sim_int_2;
656	assign pcout_3 = sim_int_3;
657	`endif
658	
659	// ============================================================================
660	// CONTROL AND MONITORING (fabric logic)
661	// ============================================================================
662	// (Register declarations moved above `ifndef SIMULATION for Icarus Verilog
663	//  forward-reference compatibility — see "SHARED REGISTER DECLARATIONS".)
664	
665	// Initialize
666	initial begin
667	    for (i = 0; i < STAGES; i = i + 1) begin
668	        comb[i] = 0;
669	        for (j = 0; j < COMB_DELAY; j = j + 1) begin
670	            comb_delay[i][j] = 0;
671	        end
672	    end
673	    integrator_sampled = 0;
674	    decimation_counter = 0;
675	    data_valid_delayed = 0;
676	    data_valid_comb = 0;
677	    data_valid_comb_pipe = 0;
678	    data_valid_comb_0_out = 0;
679	    output_counter = 0;
680	    max_integrator_value = 0;
681	    overflow_detected = 0;
682	    overflow_latched = 0;
683	    saturation_detected = 0;
684	    saturation_event_count = 0;
685	    sample_count = 0;
686	    max_value_monitor = 0;
687	    data_out = 0;
688	    data_out_valid = 0;
689	    abs_integrator_value = 0;
690	    temp_scaled_output = 0;
691	    temp_output = 0;
692	    sat_pos = 0;
693	    sat_neg = 0;
694	    temp_output_pipe = 0;
695	    data_out_valid_pipe = 0;
696	    comb_overflow_latched = 0;
697	    comb_saturation_detected = 0;
698	    comb_saturation_event_count = 0;
699	end
700	
701	// Decimation control + monitoring (integrators are now DSP48E1 instances)
702	// Sync reset: enables FDRE inference for better timing at 400 MHz.
703	// Reset is already synchronous to clk via reset synchronizer in parent module.
704	always @(posedge clk) begin
705	    if (!reset_n) begin
706	        integrator_sampled <= 0;
707	        decimation_counter <= 0;
708	        data_valid_delayed <= 0;
709	        max_integrator_value <= 0;
710	        overflow_detected <= 0;
711	        sample_count <= 0;
712	        abs_integrator_value <= 0;
713	        overflow_latched <= 0;
714	        saturation_detected <= 0;
715	        saturation_event_count <= 0;
716	        max_value_monitor <= 0;
717	        output_counter <= 0;
718	    end else begin
719	        // Monitor control
720	        if (reset_monitors) begin
721	            overflow_latched <= 0;
722	            saturation_detected <= 0;
723	            max_integrator_value <= 0;
724	            max_value_monitor <= 0;
725	            saturation_event_count <= 0;
726	        end
727	
728	        if (data_valid) begin
729	            sample_count <= sample_count + 1;
730	
731	            // Monitor integrator_0 magnitude (read DSP P output)
732	            abs_integrator_value <= (p_out_0[ACC_WIDTH-1]) ? -$signed(p_out_0) : $signed(p_out_0);
733	
734	            if (abs_integrator_value > max_integrator_value) begin
735	                max_integrator_value <= abs_integrator_value;
736	                max_value_monitor <= abs_integrator_value[27:20];
737	            end
738	
739	            // Decimation control
740	            if (decimation_counter == DECIMATION - 1) begin
741	                decimation_counter <= 0;
742	                data_valid_delayed <= 1;
743	                output_counter <= output_counter + 1;
744	                // Capture integrator_4 output, truncate to comb width
745	                integrator_sampled <= p_out_4[COMB_WIDTH-1:0];
746	            end else begin
747	                decimation_counter <= decimation_counter + 1;
748	                data_valid_delayed <= 0;
749	            end
750	        end else begin
751	            data_valid_delayed <= 0;
752	            overflow_detected <= 1'b0;
753	        end
754	    end
755	end
756	
757	// Pipeline the valid signal for comb section
758	// Sync reset: matches decimation control block reset style.
759	always @(posedge clk) begin
760	    if (!reset_n) begin
761	        data_valid_comb <= 0;
762	        data_valid_comb_pipe <= 0;
763	        data_valid_comb_0_out <= 0;
764	        integrator_sampled_comb <= 0;
765	    end else begin
766	        data_valid_comb <= data_valid_delayed;
767	        data_valid_comb_pipe <= data_valid_comb;
768	        // data_valid_comb_0_out is delayed 1 cycle from data_valid_comb_pipe
769	        // to account for CREG+AREG+BREG pipeline in comb_0_dsp.
770	        // When data_valid_comb_0_out fires, comb_0_p_out (DSP48 PREG)
771	        // contains the valid comb[0] result.
772	        data_valid_comb_0_out <= data_valid_comb_pipe;
773	        integrator_sampled_comb <= integrator_sampled;
774	    end
775	end
776	
777	// Enhanced comb section with scaling and saturation monitoring
778	// Sync reset: converts FDCE → FDRE for all comb registers. This eliminates
779	// async-clear routing overhead and enables DSP48E1 absorption of the 28-bit
780	// subtracts via Vivado's use_dsp inference. The comb subtraction was the
781	// design-wide critical path in Build 9 (8 logic levels of CARRY4 at 400 MHz,
782	// WNS = +0.128ns). DSP48E1 ALU performs 48-bit add/subtract in a single
783	// cycle with zero fabric logic, targeting WNS > +1.0ns.
784	//
785	// COMB STAGE 0: Explicit DSP48E1 with CREG=1 (comb_0_dsp instance above).
786	//   - comb[0] is driven by comb_0_p_out[COMB_WIDTH-1:0] (DSP48 P register)
787	//   - comb_delay[0][0] still captures integrator_sampled_comb in fabric
788	//   - Valid signal for stages 1-4 is data_valid_comb_0_out (delayed by 1 cycle
789	//     from data_valid_comb_pipe to match CREG+AREG+BREG pipeline latency)
790	//
791	// COMB STAGES 1-4: Inferred DSP48E1 via (* use_dsp = "yes" *) attribute.
792	//   - Each stage: comb[i] = comb[i-1] - comb_delay[i][last]
793	
794	always @(posedge clk) begin
795	    if (!reset_n) begin
796	        for (i = 0; i < STAGES; i = i + 1) begin
797	            comb[i] <= 0;
798	            for (j = 0; j < COMB_DELAY; j = j + 1) begin
799	                comb_delay[i][j] <= 0;
800	            end
801	        end
802	        data_out <= 0;
803	        data_out_valid <= 0;
804	        temp_scaled_output <= 0;
805	        temp_output <= 0;
806	        sat_pos <= 0;
807	        sat_neg <= 0;
808	        temp_output_pipe <= 0;
809	        data_out_valid_pipe <= 0;
810	        comb_overflow_latched <= 0;
811	        comb_saturation_detected <= 0;
812	        comb_saturation_event_count <= 0;
813	    end else begin
814	        if (reset_monitors) begin
815	            comb_overflow_latched <= 0;
816	            comb_saturation_detected <= 0;
817	            comb_saturation_event_count <= 0;
818	        end
819	
820	        // ---- Comb Stage 0: delay line update + DSP48 output capture ----
821	        // comb_delay[0][0] captures integrator_sampled_comb on the SAME cycle
822	        // as the DSP48 input registers (CREG/AREG/BREG), so they see the
823	        // same value. The DSP48 PREG output appears 1 cycle later.
824	        if (data_valid_comb_pipe) begin
825	            for (j = COMB_DELAY-1; j > 0; j = j - 1) begin
826	                comb_delay[0][j] <= comb_delay[0][j-1];
827	            end
828	            comb_delay[0][0] <= integrator_sampled_comb;
829	        end
830	
831	        // ---- Comb Stage 0 result: from explicit DSP48E1 ----
832	        // comb_0_dsp PREG output is valid 1 cycle after data_valid_comb_pipe.
833	        // We capture it into comb[0] on data_valid_comb_0_out so comb stages
834	        // 1-4 can read it.
835	        if (data_valid_comb_0_out) begin
836	            comb[0] <= comb_0_p_out[COMB_WIDTH-1:0];
837	        end
838	
839	        // ---- Comb Stages 1-4: inferred DSP48E1 subtracts ----
840	        // These fire on data_valid_comb_0_out (when comb[0] is valid).
841	        if (data_valid_comb_0_out) begin
842	            for (i = 1; i < STAGES; i = i + 1) begin
843	                comb[i] <= comb[i-1] - comb_delay[i][COMB_DELAY-1];
844	                for (j = COMB_DELAY-1; j > 0; j = j - 1) begin
845	                    comb_delay[i][j] <= comb_delay[i][j-1];
846	                end
847	                comb_delay[i][0] <= comb[i-1];
848	            end
849	
850	            // Gain = (4^5) = 1024 = 2^10, scale by 2^10 to normalize
851	            temp_scaled_output <= comb[STAGES-1] >>> 10;
852	            temp_output <= temp_scaled_output[17:0];
853	
854	            // Pipeline Stage 2: Register saturation comparison flags
855	            sat_pos <= (temp_scaled_output > 131071);
856	            sat_neg <= (temp_scaled_output < -131072);
857	            temp_output_pipe <= temp_scaled_output[17:0];
858	            data_out_valid_pipe <= 1;
859	        end else begin
860	            data_out_valid_pipe <= 0;
861	        end
862	
863	        // Pipeline Stage 3: MUX from registered comparison flags
864	        if (data_out_valid_pipe) begin
865	            if (sat_pos) begin
866	                data_out <= 131071;
867	                comb_overflow_latched <= 1'b1;
868	                comb_saturation_detected <= 1'b1;
869	                comb_saturation_event_count <= comb_saturation_event_count + 1;
870	                `ifdef SIMULATION
871	                $display("CIC_OUTPUT_SAT: TRUE Positive saturation, final_out=%d", 131071);
872	                `endif
873	            end else if (sat_neg) begin
874	                data_out <= -131072;
875	                comb_overflow_latched <= 1'b1;
876	                comb_saturation_detected <= 1'b1;
877	                comb_saturation_event_count <= comb_saturation_event_count + 1;
878	                `ifdef SIMULATION
879	                $display("CIC_OUTPUT_SAT: TRUE Negative saturation, final_out=%d", -131072);
880	                `endif
881	            end else begin
882	                data_out <= temp_output_pipe;
883	                comb_overflow_latched <= 1'b0;
884	                comb_saturation_detected <= 1'b0;
885	            end
886	
887	            data_out_valid <= 1;
888	        end else begin
889	            data_out_valid <= 0;
890	        end
891	    end
892	end
893	
894	// Continuous monitoring
895	`ifdef SIMULATION
896	always @(posedge clk) begin
897	    if (overflow_detected && sample_count < 100) begin
898	        $display("CIC_OVERFLOW: Immediate detection at sample %0d", sample_count);
899	    end
900	end
901	`endif
902	
903	endmodule
904

module cic_decimator_4x_enhanced (
    input wire clk,                 // 400MHz input clock
    input wire reset_n,
    input wire signed [17:0] data_in,  // 18-bit input
    input wire data_valid,
    output reg signed [17:0] data_out, // 18-bit output
    output reg data_out_valid,       // Valid at 100MHz
    // Enhanced monitoring outputs
    output reg saturation_detected,  // Latched saturation indicator
    output reg [7:0] max_value_monitor, // For gain control
    input wire reset_monitors        // Clear saturation and max value
);

parameter STAGES = 5;
parameter DECIMATION = 4;
parameter COMB_DELAY = 1;

// Accumulator width: DSP48E1 native 48-bit.
// CIC uses modular (wrapping) arithmetic so extra MSBs are harmless.
localparam ACC_WIDTH = 48;

// Comb section operates on 28-bit (18 + 5*log2(4) = 28, exact for comb range).
localparam COMB_WIDTH = 28;

// ============================================================================
// INTEGRATOR CHAIN — explicit DSP48E1 with PCOUT→PCIN cascade
// ============================================================================
// Integrator[0]: P = P + C,    C = sign_extend(data_in)  [from fabric]
// Integrator[k]: P = P + PCIN, PCIN from integrator[k-1] [dedicated cascade]
//
// The PCOUT→PCIN cascade uses dedicated silicon routing between vertically
// adjacent DSP48E1 tiles — zero fabric delay, guaranteed to meet 400+ MHz
// on 7-series regardless of speed grade.
//
// Active-high reset derived from reset_n (inverted).
// CEP (clock enable for P register) gated by data_valid.
// ============================================================================

wire reset_h = ~reset_n;  // active-high reset for DSP48E1 RSTP

// Sign-extended input for integrator_0 C port (48-bit)
wire [ACC_WIDTH-1:0] data_in_c = {{(ACC_WIDTH-18){data_in[17]}}, data_in};

// DSP48E1 cascade wires
wire [47:0] pcout_0, pcout_1, pcout_2, pcout_3;
wire [47:0] p_out_0, p_out_1, p_out_2, p_out_3, p_out_4;

// Comb stage 0 DSP48E1 output wire (CREG+AREG+BREG pipelined subtract)
wire [47:0] comb_0_p_out;

// ============================================================================
// SHARED REGISTER DECLARATIONS
// ============================================================================
// These registers are referenced by both the synthesis DSP48E1 instances
// (inside `ifndef SIMULATION) and the behavioral simulation model (inside
// `else), as well as the shared fabric logic (after `endif).
// Icarus Verilog 13.0 requires registers to be declared before their first
// use within any `ifdef branch, so we declare them here — before the
// `ifndef SIMULATION block — rather than in the post-`endif shared section.
// ============================================================================
(* keep = "true", dont_touch = "true" *) reg signed [COMB_WIDTH-1:0] integrator_sampled;
(* keep = "true", dont_touch = "true", max_fanout = 1 *) reg signed [COMB_WIDTH-1:0] integrator_sampled_comb;
(* use_dsp = "yes" *) reg signed [COMB_WIDTH-1:0] comb [0:STAGES-1];
reg signed [COMB_WIDTH-1:0] comb_delay [0:STAGES-1][0:COMB_DELAY-1];

// Pipeline valid for comb stages 1-4: delayed by 1 cycle vs comb_pipe to
// account for CREG+AREG+BREG pipeline inside comb_0_dsp (explicit DSP48E1).
// Comb[0] result appears 1 cycle after data_valid_comb_pipe.
(* keep = "true", max_fanout = 16 *) reg data_valid_comb_0_out;

// Enhanced control and monitoring
reg [1:0] decimation_counter;
(* keep = "true", max_fanout = 16 *) reg data_valid_delayed;
(* keep = "true", max_fanout = 16 *) reg data_valid_comb;
(* keep = "true", max_fanout = 16 *) reg data_valid_comb_pipe;
reg [7:0] output_counter;
reg [ACC_WIDTH-1:0] max_integrator_value;
reg overflow_detected;
reg overflow_latched;

// Diagnostic registers
reg [7:0] saturation_event_count;
reg [31:0] sample_count;

// Comb-stage saturation flags
reg comb_overflow_latched;
reg comb_saturation_detected;
reg [7:0] comb_saturation_event_count;

// Temporary signals for calculations
reg signed [ACC_WIDTH-1:0] abs_integrator_value;
reg signed [COMB_WIDTH-1:0] temp_scaled_output;
reg signed [17:0] temp_output;

// Pipeline stage for saturation comparison
reg sat_pos;
reg sat_neg;
reg signed [17:0] temp_output_pipe;
reg data_out_valid_pipe;

integer i, j;

`ifndef SIMULATION
// ============================================================================
// SYNTHESIS: Explicit DSP48E1 instances with PCOUT→PCIN cascade
// ============================================================================

// --- Integrator 0: P = P + C (accumulate sign-extended input) ---
// OPMODE = 7'b0101100: Z=P(010), Y=C(11), X=0(00) → P = P + C
// CREG=1: C port is registered inside DSP48E1. This eliminates the
// fabric→DSP C-port setup timing violation (-0.415ns in Build 6).
// The CREG adds 1 cycle of latency before data reaches the ALU.
// CEC=data_valid gates the C register to match CEP behavior.
DSP48E1 #(
    .A_INPUT            ("DIRECT"),
    .B_INPUT            ("DIRECT"),
    .USE_DPORT          ("FALSE"),
    .USE_MULT           ("NONE"),
    .AUTORESET_PATDET   ("NO_RESET"),
    .MASK               (48'h3FFFFFFFFFFF),
    .PATTERN             (48'h000000000000),
    .SEL_MASK           ("MASK"),
    .SEL_PATTERN        ("PATTERN"),
    .USE_PATTERN_DETECT ("NO_PATDET"),
    .ACASCREG           (0),
    .ADREG              (0),
    .ALUMODEREG         (0),
    .AREG               (0),
    .BCASCREG           (0),
    .BREG               (0),
    .CARRYINREG         (0),
    .CARRYINSELREG      (0),
    .CREG               (1),       // C port registered inside DSP — eliminates fabric→DSP setup path
    .DREG               (0),
    .INMODEREG          (0),
    .MREG               (0),
    .OPMODEREG          (0),
    .PREG               (1)        // P register enabled (accumulator)
) integrator_0_dsp (
    .CLK                (clk),
    .A                  (30'd0),
    .B                  (18'd0),
    .C                  (data_in_c),
    .D                  (25'd0),
    .CARRYIN            (1'b0),
    .CARRYINSEL         (3'b000),
    .OPMODE             (7'b0101100),  // P = P + C
    .ALUMODE            (4'b0000),     // Z + (X + Y + CIN)
    .INMODE             (5'b00000),
    .CEA1               (1'b0),
    .CEA2               (1'b0),
    .CEB1               (1'b0),
    .CEB2               (1'b0),
    .CEC                (data_valid),  // Register C when data is valid (CREG=1)
    .CED                (1'b0),
    .CEM                (1'b0),
    .CEP                (data_valid),  // Accumulate only when data is valid
    .CEAD               (1'b0),
    .CEALUMODE          (1'b0),
    .CECTRL             (1'b0),
    .CECARRYIN          (1'b0),
    .CEINMODE           (1'b0),
    .RSTP               (reset_h),
    .RSTA               (1'b0),
    .RSTB               (1'b0),
    .RSTC               (reset_h),     // Reset C register (CREG=1) on reset
    .RSTD               (1'b0),
    .RSTM               (1'b0),
    .RSTALLCARRYIN      (1'b0),
    .RSTALUMODE         (1'b0),
    .RSTCTRL            (1'b0),
    .RSTINMODE          (1'b0),
    .P                  (p_out_0),
    .PCOUT              (pcout_0),
    .ACOUT              (),
    .BCOUT              (),
    .CARRYCASCOUT       (),
    .CARRYOUT           (),
    .MULTSIGNOUT        (),
    .OVERFLOW           (),
    .PATTERNBDETECT     (),
    .PATTERNDETECT      (),
    .UNDERFLOW          ()
);

// --- Integrator 1: P = P + PCIN (cascade from integrator_0) ---
// OPMODE = 7'b0010010: Z=PCIN(001), Y=0(00), X=P(10) → P = P + PCIN
DSP48E1 #(
    .A_INPUT            ("DIRECT"),
    .B_INPUT            ("DIRECT"),
    .USE_DPORT          ("FALSE"),
    .USE_MULT           ("NONE"),
    .AUTORESET_PATDET   ("NO_RESET"),
    .MASK               (48'h3FFFFFFFFFFF),
    .PATTERN             (48'h000000000000),
    .SEL_MASK           ("MASK"),
    .SEL_PATTERN        ("PATTERN"),
    .USE_PATTERN_DETECT ("NO_PATDET"),
    .ACASCREG           (0),
    .ADREG              (0),
    .ALUMODEREG         (0),
    .AREG               (0),
    .BCASCREG           (0),
    .BREG               (0),
    .CARRYINREG         (0),
    .CARRYINSELREG      (0),
    .CREG               (0),
    .DREG               (0),
    .INMODEREG          (0),
    .MREG               (0),
    .OPMODEREG          (0),
    .PREG               (1)
) integrator_1_dsp (
    .CLK                (clk),
    .A                  (30'd0),
    .B                  (18'd0),
    .C                  (48'd0),
    .D                  (25'd0),
    .PCIN               (pcout_0),
    .CARRYIN            (1'b0),
    .CARRYINSEL         (3'b000),
    .OPMODE             (7'b0010010),  // P = P + PCIN
    .ALUMODE            (4'b0000),
    .INMODE             (5'b00000),
    .CEA1               (1'b0),
    .CEA2               (1'b0),
    .CEB1               (1'b0),
    .CEB2               (1'b0),
    .CEC                (1'b0),
    .CED                (1'b0),
    .CEM                (1'b0),
    .CEP                (data_valid),
    .CEAD               (1'b0),
    .CEALUMODE          (1'b0),
    .CECTRL             (1'b0),
    .CECARRYIN          (1'b0),
    .CEINMODE           (1'b0),
    .RSTP               (reset_h),
    .RSTA               (1'b0),
    .RSTB               (1'b0),
    .RSTC               (1'b0),
    .RSTD               (1'b0),
    .RSTM               (1'b0),
    .RSTALLCARRYIN      (1'b0),
    .RSTALUMODE         (1'b0),
    .RSTCTRL            (1'b0),
    .RSTINMODE          (1'b0),
    .P                  (p_out_1),
    .PCOUT              (pcout_1),
    .ACOUT              (),
    .BCOUT              (),
    .CARRYCASCOUT       (),
    .CARRYOUT           (),
    .MULTSIGNOUT        (),
    .OVERFLOW           (),
    .PATTERNBDETECT     (),
    .PATTERNDETECT      (),
    .UNDERFLOW          ()
);

// --- Integrator 2: P = P + PCIN (cascade from integrator_1) ---
DSP48E1 #(
    .A_INPUT            ("DIRECT"),
    .B_INPUT            ("DIRECT"),
    .USE_DPORT          ("FALSE"),
    .USE_MULT           ("NONE"),
    .AUTORESET_PATDET   ("NO_RESET"),
    .MASK               (48'h3FFFFFFFFFFF),
    .PATTERN             (48'h000000000000),
    .SEL_MASK           ("MASK"),
    .SEL_PATTERN        ("PATTERN"),
    .USE_PATTERN_DETECT ("NO_PATDET"),
    .ACASCREG           (0),
    .ADREG              (0),
    .ALUMODEREG         (0),
    .AREG               (0),
    .BCASCREG           (0),
    .BREG               (0),
    .CARRYINREG         (0),
    .CARRYINSELREG      (0),
    .CREG               (0),
    .DREG               (0),
    .INMODEREG          (0),
    .MREG               (0),
    .OPMODEREG          (0),
    .PREG               (1)
) integrator_2_dsp (
    .CLK                (clk),
    .A                  (30'd0),
    .B                  (18'd0),
    .C                  (48'd0),
    .D                  (25'd0),
    .PCIN               (pcout_1),
    .CARRYIN            (1'b0),
    .CARRYINSEL         (3'b000),
    .OPMODE             (7'b0010010),  // P = P + PCIN
    .ALUMODE            (4'b0000),
    .INMODE             (5'b00000),
    .CEA1               (1'b0),
    .CEA2               (1'b0),
    .CEB1               (1'b0),
    .CEB2               (1'b0),
    .CEC                (1'b0),
    .CED                (1'b0),
    .CEM                (1'b0),
    .CEP                (data_valid),
    .CEAD               (1'b0),
    .CEALUMODE          (1'b0),
    .CECTRL             (1'b0),
    .CECARRYIN          (1'b0),
    .CEINMODE           (1'b0),
    .RSTP               (reset_h),
    .RSTA               (1'b0),
    .RSTB               (1'b0),
    .RSTC               (1'b0),
    .RSTD               (1'b0),
    .RSTM               (1'b0),
    .RSTALLCARRYIN      (1'b0),
    .RSTALUMODE         (1'b0),
    .RSTCTRL            (1'b0),
    .RSTINMODE          (1'b0),
    .P                  (p_out_2),
    .PCOUT              (pcout_2),
    .ACOUT              (),
    .BCOUT              (),
    .CARRYCASCOUT       (),
    .CARRYOUT           (),
    .MULTSIGNOUT        (),
    .OVERFLOW           (),
    .PATTERNBDETECT     (),
    .PATTERNDETECT      (),
    .UNDERFLOW          ()
);

// --- Integrator 3: P = P + PCIN (cascade from integrator_2) ---
DSP48E1 #(
    .A_INPUT            ("DIRECT"),
    .B_INPUT            ("DIRECT"),
    .USE_DPORT          ("FALSE"),
    .USE_MULT           ("NONE"),
    .AUTORESET_PATDET   ("NO_RESET"),
    .MASK               (48'h3FFFFFFFFFFF),
    .PATTERN             (48'h000000000000),
    .SEL_MASK           ("MASK"),
    .SEL_PATTERN        ("PATTERN"),
    .USE_PATTERN_DETECT ("NO_PATDET"),
    .ACASCREG           (0),
    .ADREG              (0),
    .ALUMODEREG         (0),
    .AREG               (0),
    .BCASCREG           (0),
    .BREG               (0),
    .CARRYINREG         (0),
    .CARRYINSELREG      (0),
    .CREG               (0),
    .DREG               (0),
    .INMODEREG          (0),
    .MREG               (0),
    .OPMODEREG          (0),
    .PREG               (1)
) integrator_3_dsp (
    .CLK                (clk),
    .A                  (30'd0),
    .B                  (18'd0),
    .C                  (48'd0),
    .D                  (25'd0),
    .PCIN               (pcout_2),
    .CARRYIN            (1'b0),
    .CARRYINSEL         (3'b000),
    .OPMODE             (7'b0010010),  // P = P + PCIN
    .ALUMODE            (4'b0000),
    .INMODE             (5'b00000),
    .CEA1               (1'b0),
    .CEA2               (1'b0),
    .CEB1               (1'b0),
    .CEB2               (1'b0),
    .CEC                (1'b0),
    .CED                (1'b0),
    .CEM                (1'b0),
    .CEP                (data_valid),
    .CEAD               (1'b0),
    .CEALUMODE          (1'b0),
    .CECTRL             (1'b0),
    .CECARRYIN          (1'b0),
    .CEINMODE           (1'b0),
    .RSTP               (reset_h),
    .RSTA               (1'b0),
    .RSTB               (1'b0),
    .RSTC               (1'b0),
    .RSTD               (1'b0),
    .RSTM               (1'b0),
    .RSTALLCARRYIN      (1'b0),
    .RSTALUMODE         (1'b0),
    .RSTCTRL            (1'b0),
    .RSTINMODE          (1'b0),
    .P                  (p_out_3),
    .PCOUT              (pcout_3),
    .ACOUT              (),
    .BCOUT              (),
    .CARRYCASCOUT       (),
    .CARRYOUT           (),
    .MULTSIGNOUT        (),
    .OVERFLOW           (),
    .PATTERNBDETECT     (),
    .PATTERNDETECT      (),
    .UNDERFLOW          ()
);

// --- Integrator 4: P = P + PCIN (cascade from integrator_3) ---
// No PCOUT needed (last stage in cascade)
DSP48E1 #(
    .A_INPUT            ("DIRECT"),
    .B_INPUT            ("DIRECT"),
    .USE_DPORT          ("FALSE"),
    .USE_MULT           ("NONE"),
    .AUTORESET_PATDET   ("NO_RESET"),
    .MASK               (48'h3FFFFFFFFFFF),
    .PATTERN             (48'h000000000000),
    .SEL_MASK           ("MASK"),
    .SEL_PATTERN        ("PATTERN"),
    .USE_PATTERN_DETECT ("NO_PATDET"),
    .ACASCREG           (0),
    .ADREG              (0),
    .ALUMODEREG         (0),
    .AREG               (0),
    .BCASCREG           (0),
    .BREG               (0),
    .CARRYINREG         (0),
    .CARRYINSELREG      (0),
    .CREG               (0),
    .DREG               (0),
    .INMODEREG          (0),
    .MREG               (0),
    .OPMODEREG          (0),
    .PREG               (1)
) integrator_4_dsp (
    .CLK                (clk),
    .A                  (30'd0),
    .B                  (18'd0),
    .C                  (48'd0),
    .D                  (25'd0),
    .PCIN               (pcout_3),
    .CARRYIN            (1'b0),
    .CARRYINSEL         (3'b000),
    .OPMODE             (7'b0010010),  // P = P + PCIN
    .ALUMODE            (4'b0000),
    .INMODE             (5'b00000),
    .CEA1               (1'b0),
    .CEA2               (1'b0),
    .CEB1               (1'b0),
    .CEB2               (1'b0),
    .CEC                (1'b0),
    .CED                (1'b0),
    .CEM                (1'b0),
    .CEP                (data_valid),
    .CEAD               (1'b0),
    .CEALUMODE          (1'b0),
    .CECTRL             (1'b0),
    .CECARRYIN          (1'b0),
    .CEINMODE           (1'b0),
    .RSTP               (reset_h),
    .RSTA               (1'b0),
    .RSTB               (1'b0),
    .RSTC               (1'b0),
    .RSTD               (1'b0),
    .RSTM               (1'b0),
    .RSTALLCARRYIN      (1'b0),
    .RSTALUMODE         (1'b0),
    .RSTCTRL            (1'b0),
    .RSTINMODE          (1'b0),
    .P                  (p_out_4),
    .PCOUT              (),
    .ACOUT              (),
    .BCOUT              (),
    .CARRYCASCOUT       (),
    .CARRYOUT           (),
    .MULTSIGNOUT        (),
    .OVERFLOW           (),
    .PATTERNBDETECT     (),
    .PATTERNDETECT      (),
    .UNDERFLOW          ()
);

// ============================================================================
// COMB STAGE 0 — Explicit DSP48E1 with CREG=1 for Critical Path Fix
// ============================================================================
// Build 18 critical path: integrator_sampled_comb_reg → comb_reg[0]/C[38]
//   WNS = +0.062 ns, data path = 1.022 ns (0.379 logic + 0.643 route)
//
// By enabling CREG=1 (+ AREG=1, BREG=1), the fabric register
// integrator_sampled_comb is absorbed into the DSP48's internal C pipeline
// register, eliminating the 0.643 ns fabric→DSP routing delay entirely.
// The DSP48 performs: P = C_reg - {A_reg, B_reg}  (i.e., subtract)
//
// Latency: +1 cycle vs. the old inferred comb[0]. This is accounted for
// by the data_valid_comb_0_out signal, which delays the valid for stages 1-4.
//
// C-port = sign-extended integrator_sampled_comb (28→48 bits)
// A:B    = sign-extended comb_delay[0][0] (28→48 bits)
// OPMODE = 7'b0110011: Z=C(011), Y=0(00), X=A:B(11)
// ALUMODE= 4'b0011:    Z - (X + Y + CIN) = C - A:B
//
// The comb_delay[0][0] register stays in fabric (captures
// integrator_sampled_comb at the same time as the C register, unchanged).
// Comb stages 1-4 remain inferred with (* use_dsp = "yes" *).

// Sign-extended inputs for comb_0 DSP48E1
wire [47:0] comb_0_c_in = {{(48-COMB_WIDTH){integrator_sampled_comb[COMB_WIDTH-1]}},
                            integrator_sampled_comb};
wire [47:0] comb_0_ab_in = {{(48-COMB_WIDTH){comb_delay[0][COMB_DELAY-1][COMB_WIDTH-1]}},
                             comb_delay[0][COMB_DELAY-1]};

DSP48E1 #(
    .A_INPUT            ("DIRECT"),
    .B_INPUT            ("DIRECT"),
    .USE_DPORT          ("FALSE"),
    .USE_MULT           ("NONE"),
    .AUTORESET_PATDET   ("NO_RESET"),
    .MASK               (48'h3FFFFFFFFFFF),
    .PATTERN             (48'h000000000000),
    .SEL_MASK           ("MASK"),
    .SEL_PATTERN        ("PATTERN"),
    .USE_PATTERN_DETECT ("NO_PATDET"),
    .ACASCREG           (1),       // A cascade register matches AREG
    .ADREG              (0),
    .ALUMODEREG         (0),
    .AREG               (1),       // A-port registered — eliminates fabric routing
    .BCASCREG           (1),       // B cascade register matches BREG
    .BREG               (1),       // B-port registered — eliminates fabric routing
    .CARRYINREG         (0),
    .CARRYINSELREG      (0),
    .CREG               (1),       // *** KEY: C-port registered inside DSP48 ***
                                   // Absorbs integrator_sampled_comb FDRE, eliminates
                                   // 0.643 ns fabric→DSP C-port routing delay.
    .DREG               (0),
    .INMODEREG          (0),
    .MREG               (0),
    .OPMODEREG          (0),
    .PREG               (1)        // P register enabled (output pipeline)
) comb_0_dsp (
    .CLK                (clk),
    // A:B = sign-extended comb_delay[0][last] (subtrahend)
    .A                  (comb_0_ab_in[47:18]),   // Upper 30 bits
    .B                  (comb_0_ab_in[17:0]),    // Lower 18 bits
    .C                  (comb_0_c_in),           // integrator_sampled_comb (minuend)
    .D                  (25'd0),
    .CARRYIN            (1'b0),
    .CARRYINSEL         (3'b000),
    .OPMODE             (7'b0110011),  // Z=C, Y=0, X=A:B → ALU input = C, A:B
    .ALUMODE            (4'b0011),     // Z - (X+Y+CIN) = C - A:B
    .INMODE             (5'b00000),
    .CEA1               (1'b0),
    .CEA2               (data_valid_comb_pipe),  // Load A register when valid
    .CEB1               (1'b0),
    .CEB2               (data_valid_comb_pipe),  // Load B register when valid
    .CEC                (data_valid_comb_pipe),  // Load C register when valid
    .CED                (1'b0),
    .CEM                (1'b0),
    .CEP                (1'b1),        // Always propagate — P updates 1 cycle after
                                       // input registers are loaded
    .CEAD               (1'b0),
    .CEALUMODE          (1'b0),
    .CECTRL             (1'b0),
    .CECARRYIN          (1'b0),
    .CEINMODE           (1'b0),
    .RSTP               (reset_h),
    .RSTA               (reset_h),
    .RSTB               (reset_h),
    .RSTC               (reset_h),
    .RSTD               (1'b0),
    .RSTM               (1'b0),
    .RSTALLCARRYIN      (1'b0),
    .RSTALUMODE         (1'b0),
    .RSTCTRL            (1'b0),
    .RSTINMODE          (1'b0),
    .P                  (comb_0_p_out),
    .PCOUT              (),
    .ACOUT              (),
    .BCOUT              (),
    .CARRYCASCOUT       (),
    .CARRYOUT           (),
    .MULTSIGNOUT        (),
    .OVERFLOW           (),
    .PATTERNBDETECT     (),
    .PATTERNDETECT      (),
    .UNDERFLOW          ()
);

`else
// ============================================================================
// SIMULATION: Behavioral model (Icarus Verilog compatible)
// ============================================================================
// Functionally identical: each integrator is P <= P + input, gated by data_valid.
// integrator_0 adds sign-extended data_in; stages 1-4 add previous stage output.
//
// CREG=1 on integrator_0: The C-port register adds 1 cycle of latency.
// data_in_c_delayed models this: on cycle N with data_valid, the DSP's C register
// captures data_in_c(N), but the ALU uses the PREVIOUS C register value.
// So sim_int_0 accumulates data_in_c_delayed (1 cycle behind data_in_c).
// ============================================================================
reg signed [ACC_WIDTH-1:0] sim_int_0, sim_int_1, sim_int_2, sim_int_3, sim_int_4;
reg signed [ACC_WIDTH-1:0] data_in_c_delayed;  // Models CREG=1 on integrator_0

// Comb_0 DSP48E1 behavioral model (models CREG+AREG+BREG+PREG pipeline)
// In simulation there is no DSP48E1 primitive, so we model the 4-stage pipe:
//   Stage 1 (CREG/AREG/BREG): capture C and A:B inputs (on data_valid_comb_pipe)
//   Stage 2 (PREG): P = C_reg - AB_reg (always, like CEP=1 in synthesis)
reg signed [COMB_WIDTH-1:0] sim_comb_0_c_reg;   // Models CREG
reg signed [COMB_WIDTH-1:0] sim_comb_0_ab_reg;  // Models AREG+BREG (combined)
reg signed [47:0] sim_comb_0_p_reg;              // Models PREG

always @(posedge clk) begin
    if (reset_h) begin
        sim_int_0 <= 0;
        sim_int_1 <= 0;
        sim_int_2 <= 0;
        sim_int_3 <= 0;
        sim_int_4 <= 0;
        data_in_c_delayed <= 0;
        sim_comb_0_c_reg <= 0;
        sim_comb_0_ab_reg <= 0;
        sim_comb_0_p_reg <= 0;
    end else begin
        if (data_valid) begin
            // CREG pipeline: capture current data, use previous
            data_in_c_delayed <= $signed(data_in_c);
            sim_int_0 <= sim_int_0 + data_in_c_delayed;
            sim_int_1 <= sim_int_1 + sim_int_0;
            sim_int_2 <= sim_int_2 + sim_int_1;
            sim_int_3 <= sim_int_3 + sim_int_2;
            sim_int_4 <= sim_int_4 + sim_int_3;
        end
        // Comb_0 DSP48 behavioral model:
        // CREG/AREG/BREG load on data_valid_comb_pipe (like CEC/CEA2/CEB2)
        if (data_valid_comb_pipe) begin
            sim_comb_0_c_reg <= integrator_sampled_comb;
            sim_comb_0_ab_reg <= comb_delay[0][COMB_DELAY-1];
        end
        // PREG always updates (CEP=1): P = C_reg - AB_reg
        sim_comb_0_p_reg <= {{(48-COMB_WIDTH){sim_comb_0_c_reg[COMB_WIDTH-1]}}, sim_comb_0_c_reg}
                          - {{(48-COMB_WIDTH){sim_comb_0_ab_reg[COMB_WIDTH-1]}}, sim_comb_0_ab_reg};
    end
end

assign comb_0_p_out = sim_comb_0_p_reg;

assign p_out_0 = sim_int_0;
assign p_out_1 = sim_int_1;
assign p_out_2 = sim_int_2;
assign p_out_3 = sim_int_3;
assign p_out_4 = sim_int_4;
// pcout wires unused in simulation
assign pcout_0 = sim_int_0;
assign pcout_1 = sim_int_1;
assign pcout_2 = sim_int_2;
assign pcout_3 = sim_int_3;
`endif

// ============================================================================
// CONTROL AND MONITORING (fabric logic)
// ============================================================================
// (Register declarations moved above `ifndef SIMULATION for Icarus Verilog
//  forward-reference compatibility — see "SHARED REGISTER DECLARATIONS".)

// Initialize
initial begin
    for (i = 0; i < STAGES; i = i + 1) begin
        comb[i] = 0;
        for (j = 0; j < COMB_DELAY; j = j + 1) begin
            comb_delay[i][j] = 0;
        end
    end
    integrator_sampled = 0;
    decimation_counter = 0;
    data_valid_delayed = 0;
    data_valid_comb = 0;
    data_valid_comb_pipe = 0;
    data_valid_comb_0_out = 0;
    output_counter = 0;
    max_integrator_value = 0;
    overflow_detected = 0;
    overflow_latched = 0;
    saturation_detected = 0;
    saturation_event_count = 0;
    sample_count = 0;
    max_value_monitor = 0;
    data_out = 0;
    data_out_valid = 0;
    abs_integrator_value = 0;
    temp_scaled_output = 0;
    temp_output = 0;
    sat_pos = 0;
    sat_neg = 0;
    temp_output_pipe = 0;
    data_out_valid_pipe = 0;
    comb_overflow_latched = 0;
    comb_saturation_detected = 0;
    comb_saturation_event_count = 0;
end

// Decimation control + monitoring (integrators are now DSP48E1 instances)
// Sync reset: enables FDRE inference for better timing at 400 MHz.
// Reset is already synchronous to clk via reset synchronizer in parent module.
always @(posedge clk) begin
    if (!reset_n) begin
        integrator_sampled <= 0;
        decimation_counter <= 0;
        data_valid_delayed <= 0;
        max_integrator_value <= 0;
        overflow_detected <= 0;
        sample_count <= 0;
        abs_integrator_value <= 0;
        overflow_latched <= 0;
        saturation_detected <= 0;
        saturation_event_count <= 0;
        max_value_monitor <= 0;
        output_counter <= 0;
    end else begin
        // Monitor control
        if (reset_monitors) begin
            overflow_latched <= 0;
            saturation_detected <= 0;
            max_integrator_value <= 0;
            max_value_monitor <= 0;
            saturation_event_count <= 0;
        end

        if (data_valid) begin
            sample_count <= sample_count + 1;

            // Monitor integrator_0 magnitude (read DSP P output)
            abs_integrator_value <= (p_out_0[ACC_WIDTH-1]) ? -$signed(p_out_0) : $signed(p_out_0);

            if (abs_integrator_value > max_integrator_value) begin
                max_integrator_value <= abs_integrator_value;
                max_value_monitor <= abs_integrator_value[27:20];
            end

            // Decimation control
            if (decimation_counter == DECIMATION - 1) begin
                decimation_counter <= 0;
                data_valid_delayed <= 1;
                output_counter <= output_counter + 1;
                // Capture integrator_4 output, truncate to comb width
                integrator_sampled <= p_out_4[COMB_WIDTH-1:0];
            end else begin
                decimation_counter <= decimation_counter + 1;
                data_valid_delayed <= 0;
            end
        end else begin
            data_valid_delayed <= 0;
            overflow_detected <= 1'b0;
        end
    end
end

// Pipeline the valid signal for comb section
// Sync reset: matches decimation control block reset style.
always @(posedge clk) begin
    if (!reset_n) begin
        data_valid_comb <= 0;
        data_valid_comb_pipe <= 0;
        data_valid_comb_0_out <= 0;
        integrator_sampled_comb <= 0;
    end else begin
        data_valid_comb <= data_valid_delayed;
        data_valid_comb_pipe <= data_valid_comb;
        // data_valid_comb_0_out is delayed 1 cycle from data_valid_comb_pipe
        // to account for CREG+AREG+BREG pipeline in comb_0_dsp.
        // When data_valid_comb_0_out fires, comb_0_p_out (DSP48 PREG)
        // contains the valid comb[0] result.
        data_valid_comb_0_out <= data_valid_comb_pipe;
        integrator_sampled_comb <= integrator_sampled;
    end
end

// Enhanced comb section with scaling and saturation monitoring
// Sync reset: converts FDCE → FDRE for all comb registers. This eliminates
// async-clear routing overhead and enables DSP48E1 absorption of the 28-bit
// subtracts via Vivado's use_dsp inference. The comb subtraction was the
// design-wide critical path in Build 9 (8 logic levels of CARRY4 at 400 MHz,
// WNS = +0.128ns). DSP48E1 ALU performs 48-bit add/subtract in a single
// cycle with zero fabric logic, targeting WNS > +1.0ns.
//
// COMB STAGE 0: Explicit DSP48E1 with CREG=1 (comb_0_dsp instance above).
//   - comb[0] is driven by comb_0_p_out[COMB_WIDTH-1:0] (DSP48 P register)
//   - comb_delay[0][0] still captures integrator_sampled_comb in fabric
//   - Valid signal for stages 1-4 is data_valid_comb_0_out (delayed by 1 cycle
//     from data_valid_comb_pipe to match CREG+AREG+BREG pipeline latency)
//
// COMB STAGES 1-4: Inferred DSP48E1 via (* use_dsp = "yes" *) attribute.
//   - Each stage: comb[i] = comb[i-1] - comb_delay[i][last]

always @(posedge clk) begin
    if (!reset_n) begin
        for (i = 0; i < STAGES; i = i + 1) begin
            comb[i] <= 0;
            for (j = 0; j < COMB_DELAY; j = j + 1) begin
                comb_delay[i][j] <= 0;
            end
        end
        data_out <= 0;
        data_out_valid <= 0;
        temp_scaled_output <= 0;
        temp_output <= 0;
        sat_pos <= 0;
        sat_neg <= 0;
        temp_output_pipe <= 0;
        data_out_valid_pipe <= 0;
        comb_overflow_latched <= 0;
        comb_saturation_detected <= 0;
        comb_saturation_event_count <= 0;
    end else begin
        if (reset_monitors) begin
            comb_overflow_latched <= 0;
            comb_saturation_detected <= 0;
            comb_saturation_event_count <= 0;
        end

        // ---- Comb Stage 0: delay line update + DSP48 output capture ----
        // comb_delay[0][0] captures integrator_sampled_comb on the SAME cycle
        // as the DSP48 input registers (CREG/AREG/BREG), so they see the
        // same value. The DSP48 PREG output appears 1 cycle later.
        if (data_valid_comb_pipe) begin
            for (j = COMB_DELAY-1; j > 0; j = j - 1) begin
                comb_delay[0][j] <= comb_delay[0][j-1];
            end
            comb_delay[0][0] <= integrator_sampled_comb;
        end

        // ---- Comb Stage 0 result: from explicit DSP48E1 ----
        // comb_0_dsp PREG output is valid 1 cycle after data_valid_comb_pipe.
        // We capture it into comb[0] on data_valid_comb_0_out so comb stages
        // 1-4 can read it.
        if (data_valid_comb_0_out) begin
            comb[0] <= comb_0_p_out[COMB_WIDTH-1:0];
        end

        // ---- Comb Stages 1-4: inferred DSP48E1 subtracts ----
        // These fire on data_valid_comb_0_out (when comb[0] is valid).
        if (data_valid_comb_0_out) begin
            for (i = 1; i < STAGES; i = i + 1) begin
                comb[i] <= comb[i-1] - comb_delay[i][COMB_DELAY-1];
                for (j = COMB_DELAY-1; j > 0; j = j - 1) begin
                    comb_delay[i][j] <= comb_delay[i][j-1];
                end
                comb_delay[i][0] <= comb[i-1];
            end

            // Gain = (4^5) = 1024 = 2^10, scale by 2^10 to normalize
            temp_scaled_output <= comb[STAGES-1] >>> 10;
            temp_output <= temp_scaled_output[17:0];

            // Pipeline Stage 2: Register saturation comparison flags
            sat_pos <= (temp_scaled_output > 131071);
            sat_neg <= (temp_scaled_output < -131072);
            temp_output_pipe <= temp_scaled_output[17:0];
            data_out_valid_pipe <= 1;
        end else begin
            data_out_valid_pipe <= 0;
        end

        // Pipeline Stage 3: MUX from registered comparison flags
        if (data_out_valid_pipe) begin
            if (sat_pos) begin
                data_out <= 131071;
                comb_overflow_latched <= 1'b1;
                comb_saturation_detected <= 1'b1;
                comb_saturation_event_count <= comb_saturation_event_count + 1;
                `ifdef SIMULATION
                $display("CIC_OUTPUT_SAT: TRUE Positive saturation, final_out=%d", 131071);
                `endif
            end else if (sat_neg) begin
                data_out <= -131072;
                comb_overflow_latched <= 1'b1;
                comb_saturation_detected <= 1'b1;
                comb_saturation_event_count <= comb_saturation_event_count + 1;
                `ifdef SIMULATION
                $display("CIC_OUTPUT_SAT: TRUE Negative saturation, final_out=%d", -131072);
                `endif
            end else begin
                data_out <= temp_output_pipe;
                comb_overflow_latched <= 1'b0;
                comb_saturation_detected <= 1'b0;
            end

            data_out_valid <= 1;
        end else begin
            data_out_valid <= 0;
        end
    end
end

// Continuous monitoring
`ifdef SIMULATION
always @(posedge clk) begin
    if (overflow_detected && sample_count < 100) begin
        $display("CIC_OVERFLOW: Immediate detection at sample %0d", sample_count);
    end
end
`endif

endmodule
